prompt:
Badge-swipe reconciliation for the science complex keeps flagging impossible events, and it is going to sink our access audit. From this month's ingests: swipes stamped in year 2103 (and one in year 0008), swipes attributed to the wrong day entirely, and intermittent aborts — NumberFormatException: For input string: "" and the occasional ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException from somewhere inside the date handling. The kicker: it ONLY happens on mornings when several door controllers upload during the same window. Replay any single controller's file by itself and every record comes out byte-perfect — the vendor has closed the ticket twice on that basis.

TestMain.java now simulates the simultaneous-upload window with known fixtures and catches the corruption on every run: `java TestMain.java` (plain JDK source launcher, no build step). Please fix the swipe library so the whole suite passes — single-controller replays, malformed-stamp rejection, AND the simultaneous windows. TestMain.java stays untouched.
